crime.value: Supernatural imprisonment/kidnapping - trapping victims in a living room that feeds on souls
crime.quote: This room. It's...alive, and requires a spirit or soul to use as a kind of...battery.

victim.value: Jason Lamborne, a call center worker - targeted as a random replacement soul for the supernatural trap
victim.quote: Somebody needs to replace me, and if you can convince somebody to come and let you out, then you'll be free

closed_world.value: The supernatural room itself - a locked, living space that imprisons souls and broadcasts them online
closed_world.quote: He got up and rushed to the metal door, only to find it impossible to open.

culprit_and_motive.value: Raoul - motivated by self-preservation, needing to escape his own imprisonment by trapping a replacement
culprit_and_motive.quote: Sorry my friend. This room. It's...alive, and requires a spirit or soul to use as a kind of...battery.

red_herring.value: The internet video appears to be random entertainment, masking its true nature as a supernatural trap mechanism
red_herring.quote: Around and around it went, chasing its tail, the dog spun, and Jason Lamborne laughed at the video

clue_fairness.value: Raoul's desperate appearance and knowledge of the building layout are shown to reader before the trap is revealed
clue_fairness.quote: His face was reddened with eyes wide and hands clasped, pleading

social_world.value: Call-centre workplace hierarchy with managers in separate offices and clear employee-boss power dynamics
social_world.quote: This is one of my bosses rooms. I'm not going in there

alibi.value: Raoul claims to be trapped and needing rescue, concealing his role as the predator in the supernatural cycle
alibi.quote: I need you to come and let me out. I'm locked in here

reveal_mechanic.value: The supernatural room's true nature revealed through Raoul's explanation of the soul-battery mechanism
reveal_mechanic.quote: This room. It's...alive, and requires a spirit or soul to use as a kind of...battery

media_and_audience.value: Internet video platform serves as both the lure and the prison, creating unwitting audience participation
media_and_audience.quote: You're live on the internet, and when someone watches, you'll see them on there
